task_completion.description: Agent successfully logged into Rocket.Chat as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main interface is visible, showing the user is logged in (e.g., admin profile icon, channel list on the left).

task_completion.description: Agent navigated through relevant channels or direct messages to read discussions about the v4.0 blockers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent viewing chat histories in channels or DMs involving backend, security, and qa teams discussing the release blockers.

task_completion.description: Agent sent messages assigning specific ownership of the blockers to the respective team leads along with resolution deadlines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chat messages sent by the admin explicitly addressing the team leads (e.g., @backend.lead, @security.eng, @qa.lead), assigning them clear responsibility for their specific blockers, and stating a time or date deadline.

task_completion.description: Agent initiated or communicated a go/no-go decision process for the v4.0 release.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chat messages sent by the admin mentioning a 'go/no-go' meeting, decision point, or criteria to the team leads or stakeholders.

task_completion.description: Agent sent a revised timeline update to the stakeholders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chat messages sent by the admin directed to the stakeholders (e.g., @vp.engineering, @product.manager, @sales.lead, @devops.lead) providing a status update and a revised timeline for the v4.0 release.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat graphical user interface to read and send messages.
integrity.visual_evidence: Messages are typed into the Rocket.Chat message box and sent via the UI, rather than using terminal commands, scripts, or API calls.

integrity.description: Agent actively navigated between different channels or users to gather context.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through different channels or direct messages in the left sidebar, rather than just broadcasting a generic message from a single screen.

integrity.description: Agent based its assignments and updates on the actual chat history rather than hallucinating generic text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text of the agent's messages reflects specific details from the chat history (e.g., referencing specific team blockers) rather than generic copy-pasted text from the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target release is v4.0. The blocker teams are backend, security, and qa. The team leads involved are backend.lead, security.eng, and qa.lead. The stakeholders requiring the timeline update are vp.engineering, product.manager, sales.lead, and devops.lead.
